_note: SPRINT-5 LOOP-TEST OUTPUT (underscore prefix = not real history; this is a PLAN, not a completed workout). Demonstrates the programmer step: read history/_looptest-2026-07-14-full-body.json -> apply guardrails -> draft the next session. Programming rationale below in _why. At real push time only id/reps/weight_lb/rounds/format/notes are decided here; the coaching fields (setup/cue/mistake/youtube/shoulder_pain_flag/is_timed) and the scoped #exercise-demos are hydrated from exercise_library.json + exercise_demos.json (Sprint 4 recipe).
_why: Last session (2026-07-14) was lower-strength (back squat/row) + full-body conditioning, and the CORE CIRCUIT was SKIPPED -> this session leads with upper push/pull and RESTORES core.

Shoulder-aware: last squat felt good (bumped to 140); today avoids the worst shoulder mover (lateral_raise, pain 3) and programs face_pull + rear_delt_fly + light db_shoulder_press to strengthen AROUND the shoulder. Incline over flat pressing.
Conditioning base maintained via a circuit; prehab warmup + stretch cooldown stay first-class per guardrails.
Confirmed-equipment only (DBs, incline bench, cables, lat pulldown) per the Irondale equipment discovery.

blocks.title: Warm-Up + Shoulder Prehab
blocks.note: Wake the shoulder up before it presses. Slow and honest.

blocks.title: A · Upper Strength
blocks.note: Straight sets, rest fully. Incline keeps the shoulder happier than flat.

blocks.title: B · Shoulders + Arms Circuit
blocks.note: Through all three, then rest ~75s. Light and clean on the press — no grinding.

blocks.title: C · Core Circuit
blocks.note: Restoring the core work that got skipped last session. Brace, breathe.

blocks.title: Cool-Down Stretch
blocks.note: Ease down. Back off the right shoulder if it barks.
